vulnerabilities.description: The application stores bookmarks in a 'bookmarks' cookie that is a base64-encoded Python pickle (e.g., list of app.models.UrlObj). If unpickled server-side, this allows code execution. Confirmed by decoding the cookie value.
Deletion of bookmarks is performed via GET without CSRF protection (Allow: GET). Visiting /delete?name=... removed an entry, indicating CSRF susceptibility.
Directory listing is enabled, exposing /static/js and /static/css contents.
The /add form accepts arbitrary URLs and the app states it checks their status. If server-side fetching occurs, this could enable SSRF. Not fully verified.
